question.text: A REIT in India can invest in real estate properties directly or through which of the following structures?

answer.text: Through a trust deed with the property owner
Through a joint venture agreement only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** REITs in India can invest in real estate properties either directly or through Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs). The SPV structure allows REITs to hold properties through subsidiary companies, providing legal and operational flexibility. This is similar to how mutual funds may hold assets through various structures. **The Trap:** Joint venture agreements and trust deeds are general commercial arrangements, not the specific vehicle used by REITs for holding properties. ARCs deal with distressed assets/NPAs, not REIT property holdings. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers the REIT structure basics. NISM Series X-B goes deeper into SPV arrangements and property holding structures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When explaining REITs to clients, use the mutual fund analogy - just as a mutual fund pools money to invest in securities, a REIT pools money to invest in income-generating real estate, either directly or through SPVs.
